# QuickSort

> QuickSort is the fastest only if we make the right decisions about pivot selection; partition algorithm; how to deal with the worst case.
 
==Partitioning==: to rearrange the array such that all items to the left of the pivot are $\le$ pivot. The pivot can move during the process.

## Algorithm：按pivot大小重排，把小的放左，大的放右
1. Partition on the leftmost item as the pivot
2. Recursively quick sort the left half.
3. Recursively quick sort the right half
### QuickSort vs MergeSort
- MergeSort稳定, merge是从中间切开，只跟下标有关
- QuickSort会取决于pivot choice
- Compare to Merge sort: on average, quick sort is   
$Θ(NlogN)$.
#### Quicksort L3S
- Always leftmost pivot selection
- Partition Algorithm: 3-scan (A three-pass (three scans) partition that splits the array into < pivot, == pivot, > pivot using an extra array.)
- Worst Case avoidance strategy: shuffle
#### Quicksort LTHS (Fix partition algorithm, faster than Mergesort if pivot good)
- Tony Hoare Partitioning: ==important pivot selection strategy==
- 选中pivot L: A[0]，
- create L and G pointers at left and right ends:
	L = pivot + 1 (hate large or equal items)
	G = 最右边 (hate small or equal items)
- Walk pointers towards each other, stopping on a hated item:
	- When both pointer stopped, swap 
	- Move pointer by one
	- When pointers cross, done walking
- Swap pivot with G

#### QuickSort 
- calculate the exact median: slower than MergeSort
- Cost to compute medians is too high
- Have to live with worst case

#### ==QuickSelect==
Goal: find the median using partitioning

## Runtime
- Best case: $Θ(NlogN)$
	-  每层工作量N：第一层partition：N
		- 第二层：N/2 + N/2
		- ...
	- 一共有几层取决于N能除以2几次，所以是logN
- Worst case: $Θ(N^2)$
- Compare to Merge sort: on average, quick sort is   
$Θ(NlogN)$.
